clusters.title: Trump vows response after Iran shoots down US helicopter
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump says Iran shot down US helicopter and vows to respond
bullet_summary: "must, of necessity, respond," President Trump said Tuesday
The two crew members were later rescued in an unprecedented operation using an unmanned drone boat, the military said
American president said Tehran downed Apache aircraft over Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
Trump also confirmed the two pilots were unharmed and safe

items.title: Trump vows response after Iran shoots down US helicopter
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump vowed on Tuesday to respond after he accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter the previous night, hours after he said negotiations to end the Middle East war were in their final stages. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: President Donald Trump vowed on Tuesday to respond after he accused Iran of shooting down a US military helicopter the previous night, hours after he said negotiations to end the Middle East war were in their final stages. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Trump says Iran shot down US helicopter and vows to respond
items.standfirst: Two crew members of the Apache helicopter that crashed following the attack were rescued by an American sea drone.
items.content: Two crew members of the Apache helicopter that crashed following the attack were rescued by an American sea drone.

items.title: Trump says Iran shot down Apache helicopter and U.S. must respond
items.standfirst: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured but the U.S. "must, of necessity, respond," President Trump said Tuesday.
items.content: The two helicopter pilots were uninjured but the U.S. "must, of necessity, respond," President Trump said Tuesday.

items.title: U.S. Apache helicopter shot down by Iran, Trump says; crew rescued by sea drone
items.standfirst: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.
items.content: The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone in the first such operation ever carried out by the U.S. military, officials told CBS News.

items.title: Trump blames Iran for downing of army helicopter and says US must respond
items.standfirst: US president says crew members are ‘safe and uninjured’ after attack near strait of Hormuz early on TuesdayMiddle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has blamed Iran for downing a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz, warning that the US would be forced to respond to the attack.The US army Apache helicopter gunship crashed off the coast of Oman early on Tuesday, according to US Central Command. The two crew members were later rescued in an unprecedented operation using an unmanned drone boat, the military said. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says crew members are ‘safe and uninjured’ after attack near strait of Hormuz early on TuesdayMiddle East crisis – live updatesDonald Trump has blamed Iran for downing a US army helicopter near the strait of Hormuz, warning that the US would be forced to respond to the attack.The US army Apache helicopter gunship crashed off the coast of Oman early on Tuesday, according to US Central Command. The two crew members were later rescued in an unprecedented operation using an unmanned drone boat, the military said. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump vows to ‘respond’ after Iran ‘shot down’ a US helicopter
items.standfirst: American president said Tehran downed Apache aircraft over Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
items.content: American president said Tehran downed Apache aircraft over Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire

items.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. Apache helicopter, crew rescued by sea drone
items.standfirst: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.
items.content: President Trump is vowing to respond after confirming that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The two crew members were rescued by a sea drone, the first rescue of its kind. CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

items.title: Trump confirms Iran shot down helicopter, says U.S. 'must' respond
items.standfirst: Trump also confirmed the two pilots were unharmed and safe.
items.content: Trump also confirmed the two pilots were unharmed and safe.(Image credit: Saul Loeb)

items.title: Trump says Iran shot down US helicopter over Hormuz, vows to respond
items.standfirst: US president says although the pilots were rescued, Washington 'must, of necessity, respond to this attack'.
items.content: US president says although the pilots were rescued, Washington 'must, of necessity, respond to this attack'.

clusters.title: NASA names 4 astronauts on the 'highly complex' Artemis III lunar training mission
clusters.neutral_headline: Nasa names next astronauts for Artemis Moon programme
bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: NASA names 4 astronauts on the 'highly complex' Artemis III lunar training mission
items.standfirst: The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.
items.content: The crew of four — NASA astronaut and commander Randy Bresnik, European Space Agency pilot Luca Parmitano, NASA mission specialists Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas — are scheduled to launch next year.(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)

items.title: NASA names 4 astronauts for next Artemis mission
items.standfirst: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.
items.content: NASA's Artemis III astronauts plan to carry out rendezvous and docking procedures with commercial moon landers being built by SpaceX and Blue Origin.

items.title: Nasa names next astronauts for Artemis Moon programme
items.standfirst: Nasa names its next Artemis crew, though they will not be walking on the Moon or even going anywhere near it.
items.content: Nasa names its next Artemis crew, though they will not be walking on the Moon or even going anywhere near it.

items.title: ‘Earth’s first starfleet’: Nasa reveals Artemis III crew and project’s next steps
items.standfirst: Luca Parmitano to pilot all-male crew of four paving way for planned first human landing on Artemis IV in 2028Jared Isaacman, the Nasa administrator, hailed the creation of “Earth’s first starfleet” on Tuesday as he revealed the Artemis III crew and details of the next stages of the space agency’s project to return humans to the moon.An Italian astronaut, Luca Parmitano of the European Space Agency (ESA), will be the pilot of the planned two-week mission to lower Earth orbit next year that will test lunar landers from private companies Blue Origin and SpaceX. Continue reading...
items.content: Luca Parmitano to pilot all-male crew of four paving way for planned first human landing on Artemis IV in 2028Jared Isaacman, the Nasa administrator, hailed the creation of “Earth’s first starfleet” on Tuesday as he revealed the Artemis III crew and details of the next stages of the space agency’s project to return humans to the moon.An Italian astronaut, Luca Parmitano of the European Space Agency (ESA), will be the pilot of the planned two-week mission to lower Earth orbit next year that will test lunar landers from private companies Blue Origin and SpaceX. Continue reading...

items.title: NASA Selects Crew for Artemis Mission to Test Moon Landers
items.standfirst: NASA announced the four astronauts who will fly to space as soon as next year to test out moon landers built by SpaceX and Blue Origin — the next stage in the agency’s ambitious Artemis program to ultimately send humans back to the lunar surface.
items.content: NASA announced the four astronauts who will fly to space as soon as next year to test out moon landers built by SpaceX and Blue Origin — the next stage in the agency’s ambitious Artemis program to ultimately send humans back to the lunar surface.

items.content: France will also bar far-right Israeli Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entry as part of the measures, which Israel condemns as "disgraceful".

items.title: France bans Israeli minister Smotrich in coordinated sanctions push
items.standfirst: France Tuesday banned Israel's far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entering the country, as part of coordinated sanctions with other countries over settler violence against Palestinians. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
items.content: France Tuesday banned Israel's far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entering the country, as part of coordinated sanctions with other countries over settler violence against Palestinians. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Six countries sanction enablers of settler violence in occupied West Bank
items.standfirst: The Western nations also say they will take further measures if Israel fails to address the situation on the ground.
items.content: The Western nations also say they will take further measures if Israel fails to address the situation on the ground.

items.title: France bans Israel’s finance minister, slams him for promoting ‘recolonisation’ of Gaza
items.standfirst: France on Tuesday banned Israel’s Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entry, slamming the far-right politician’s calls for the annexation of the occupied West Bank, new settlements in the area and the “recolonisation” of Gaza. Smotrich was among dozens of Israelis who were slapped with sanctions by France, Britain, Canada, ​Australia, New Zealand and Norway.
items.content: France on Tuesday banned Israel’s Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich from entry, slamming the far-right politician’s calls for the annexation of the occupied West Bank, new settlements in the area and the “recolonisation” of Gaza. Smotrich was among dozens of Israelis who were slapped with sanctions by France, Britain, Canada, ​Australia, New Zealand and Norway.

clusters.title: Trump immigration stance 'clashes against reality of what is needed to stage a truly global event'
clusters.neutral_headline: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup
bullet_summary: Award-winning Somali referee Omar Artan has been dropped from officiating at the World Cup after being barred from entering the United States, world football's governing body FIFA said on Monday
A FIFA spokesperson confirmed in a statement that Artan would play no part in the World Cup

items.title: Trump immigration stance 'clashes against reality of what is needed to stage a truly global event'
items.standfirst: Award-winning Somali referee Omar Artan has been dropped from officiating at the World Cup after being barred from entering the United States. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Alexander Cooley, Claire Tow Professor of Political Science at Barnard College, explains that "we're seeing the Trump's administration adherence to hard-line immigration laws clash against the reality of what you need to stage a truly global event".
items.content: Award-winning Somali referee Omar Artan has been dropped from officiating at the World Cup after being barred from entering the United States. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Alexander Cooley, Claire Tow Professor of Political Science at Barnard College, explains that "we're seeing the Trump's administration adherence to hard-line immigration laws clash against the reality of what you need to stage a truly global event".

items.title: Does referee case show Fifa has lost control of its own World Cup?
items.standfirst: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?
items.content: With referee Omar Artan denied entry to the United States and worries over staff and supporters being turned back, what does this tell us about the World Cup?

items.title: Somali referee axed from World Cup after being denied entry to US
items.standfirst: Award-winning Somali referee Omar Artan has been dropped from officiating at the World Cup after being barred from entering the United States, world football's governing body FIFA said on Monday. A FIFA spokesperson confirmed in a statement that Artan would play no part in the World Cup. Artan would have been the first Somali to referee at a World Cup.
items.content: Award-winning Somali referee Omar Artan has been dropped from officiating at the World Cup after being barred from entering the United States, world football's governing body FIFA said on Monday. A FIFA spokesperson confirmed in a statement that Artan would play no part in the World Cup. Artan would have been the first Somali to referee at a World Cup.

items.title: I have right papers and visa - barred referee Artan
items.standfirst: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".
items.content: Somali referee Omar Artan says he was subjected to an 11-hour immigration interview before being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup despite holding the "right papers" and "right visa".

items.title: Top referee to miss World Cup after US denies him entry
items.standfirst: A top Somali referee will not officiate at the World Cup after being denied entry into the US.
items.content: A top Somali referee will not officiate at the World Cup after being denied entry into the US.

clusters.title: Far right accused of seeking to foment UK-wide unrest over Belfast knife attack
clusters.neutral_headline: Far right accused of seeking to foment UK-wide unrest over Belfast knife attack

items.title: Far right accused of seeking to foment UK-wide unrest over Belfast knife attack
items.standfirst: Crowds, including people in masks and hoods, begin to gather in parts of the city after calls for mass protestsPoliticians and community leaders have accused the far right of seeking to foment unrest in Northern Ireland and across the UK after a graphic video of a knife attack in Belfast prompted calls for mass street protests against immigrants and refugees.Police charged a 30-year-old asylum seeker from Sudan with attempted murder on Tuesday night after an attack that left a man severely injured and caused widespread shock and revulsion. Continue reading...
items.content: Crowds, including people in masks and hoods, begin to gather in parts of the city after calls for mass protestsPoliticians and community leaders have accused the far right of seeking to foment unrest in Northern Ireland and across the UK after a graphic video of a knife attack in Belfast prompted calls for mass street protests against immigrants and refugees.Police charged a 30-year-old asylum seeker from Sudan with attempted murder on Tuesday night after an attack that left a man severely injured and caused widespread shock and revulsion. items.title: ‘You can never be safe’: Belfast’s Sudanese community hunkers down after grisly attack
items.standfirst: Fears grow over anti-immigration protests after asylum seeker charged with attempted murder in Northern IrelandThe Sudanese barber shop owner was at his cash register and smiling at the question, “Did he feel safe in Belfast?”, when two men strolling down the street paused at his open doorway and unleashed a sudden, shrieking howl.It ended as abruptly as it began and without saying a word the two men, white, in their 20s, wearing grey tracksuits, resumed their stroll. Continue reading...
items.content: Fears grow over anti-immigration protests after asylum seeker charged with attempted murder in Northern IrelandThe Sudanese barber shop owner was at his cash register and smiling at the question, “Did he feel safe in Belfast?”, when two men strolling down the street paused at his open doorway and unleashed a sudden, shrieking howl.It ended as abruptly as it began and without saying a word the two men, white, in their 20s, wearing grey tracksuits, resumed their stroll. Continue reading...

items.title: Belfast protesters set vehicles on fire, block roads over brutal street stabbing
items.standfirst: Protesters on Tuesday set vehicles on fire and blocked several central roads in the Northern Ireland city of Belfast after police charged a Sudanese national suspected of violently stabbing another man in the street the night before. The attack was caught on video and prompted far-right figures to call for anti-migrant protests across the United Kingdom.
items.content: Protesters on Tuesday set vehicles on fire and blocked several central roads in the Northern Ireland city of Belfast after police charged a Sudanese national suspected of violently stabbing another man in the street the night before. The attack was caught on video and prompted far-right figures to call for anti-migrant protests across the United Kingdom.

clusters.title: FCAS project failure: 'France, Germany characterised by totally different strategic cultures'
clusters.neutral_headline: France and Germany abandon joint project to build European fighter jet

items.title: FCAS project failure: 'France, Germany characterised by totally different strategic cultures'
items.standfirst: France and Germany said on Monday they had agreed to abandon a joint fighter jet programme due to disagreements between the companies involved, in a blow to European efforts to boost defence cooperation. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Jeanette Süß, Research Fellow, Study Committee on Franco-German Relations, explains that "France and Germany are characterised by totally different strategic cultures, totally different military cultures and also in their weapons systems".
items.content: France and Germany said on Monday they had agreed to abandon a joint fighter jet programme due to disagreements between the companies involved, in a blow to European efforts to boost defence cooperation. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Jeanette Süß, Research Fellow, Study Committee on Franco-German Relations, explains that "France and Germany are characterised by totally different strategic cultures, totally different military cultures and also in their weapons systems".

items.title: France and Germany abandon joint project to build European fighter jet
items.standfirst: Paris and Berlin conclude firms involved unable to agree on way forward in blow to Europe’s common defence pushFrance and Germany have concluded that the companies involved in building a joint fighter jet will not be able to reach an agreement and have abandoned the project, officials in Berlin have said in a blow to Europe’s common defence efforts.The French president, Emmanuel Macron, and the German chancellor, Friedrich Merz, had “reached the shared assessment that the companies will not be able to come together”, an official told Agence France-Presse. “They acknowledge this reality.” Continue reading...
items.content: Paris and Berlin conclude firms involved unable to agree on way forward in blow to Europe’s common defence pushFrance and Germany have concluded that the companies involved in building a joint fighter jet will not be able to reach an agreement and have abandoned the project, officials in Berlin have said in a blow to Europe’s common defence efforts.The French president, Emmanuel Macron, and the German chancellor, Friedrich Merz, had “reached the shared assessment that the companies will not be able to come together”, an official told Agence France-Presse. “They acknowledge this reality.” Continue reading...

clusters.title: Suspect in Charlotte train stabbing found incompetent for trial "at this time"

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Suspect in Charlotte train stabbing found incompetent for trial "at this time"
items.standfirst: Decarlos Brown Jr, the man accused of fatally stabbing Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte, North Carolina, light rail train, has been ruled incompetent for trial "at this time." CBS News correspondent Shanelle Kaul has more.
items.content: Decarlos Brown Jr, the man accused of fatally stabbing Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte, North Carolina, light rail train, has been ruled incompetent for trial "at this time." CBS News correspondent Shanelle Kaul has more.

items.title: US judge finds man accused of killing woman on Charlotte train incompetent to stand trial
items.standfirst: Decarlos Brown Jr to stay in custody while receiving treatment for remainder of case over Iryna Zarutska’s deathThe man accused of fatally stabbing Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte commuter train in August has been found incompetent to stand trial in federal court for now, the US attorney’s office for the western district of North Carolina said on Tuesday.Decarlos Brown Jr, 35, is accused of killing Zarutska, a 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee, on a Charlotte light rail train in a case that drew national attention after a surveillance camera video depicting the violent attack was released. Continue reading...
items.content: Decarlos Brown Jr to stay in custody while receiving treatment for remainder of case over Iryna Zarutska’s deathThe man accused of fatally stabbing Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte commuter train in August has been found incompetent to stand trial in federal court for now, the US attorney’s office for the western district of North Carolina said on Tuesday.Decarlos Brown Jr, 35, is accused of killing Zarutska, a 23-year-old Ukrainian refugee, on a Charlotte light rail train in a case that drew national attention after a surveillance camera video depicting the violent attack was released. clusters.title: Unbreakable Kim? China's Xi visits Russia-backed North Korean leader
clusters.neutral_headline: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un vow stronger ties as North Korea visit wraps up

items.title: Unbreakable Kim? China's Xi visits Russia-backed North Korean leader
items.standfirst: It takes convincing these days to get China’s leader to go abroad. Xi Jinping’s just wrapped up what was only his first foreign visit of 2026. Why Pyongyang? Hard to deduce from official readouts of that two-day state visit to neighboring North Korea. Politeness after a pair of visits by Kim Jong Un to Beijing? Celebrations of the 65th anniversary of their mutual defense pact?
items.content: It takes convincing these days to get China’s leader to go abroad. Xi Jinping’s just wrapped up what was only his first foreign visit of 2026. Why Pyongyang? Hard to deduce from official readouts of that two-day state visit to neighboring North Korea. Politeness after a pair of visits by Kim Jong Un to Beijing? Celebrations of the 65th anniversary of their mutual defense pact?

items.title: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un vow stronger ties as North Korea visit wraps up
items.standfirst: Xi has wrapped up a two-day visit to Pyongyang, his first official trip to North Korea since 2019.
items.content: Xi has wrapped up a two-day visit to Pyongyang, his first official trip to North Korea since 2019.

clusters.title: Bear captured in Japan after multi-day search as attacks rise
clusters.neutral_headline: Bear captured in Japan after multi-day search as attacks rise
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Sky News World

items.title: Bear captured in Japan after multi-day search as attacks rise
items.standfirst: A bear that roamed the streets of a Japanese city for four days, forcing mass school closures, was caught on June 9, capping a search involving dozens of hunters and police. Bear attacks have been on the rise in Japan. Last week, a bear attack in the northeastern city of Fukushima left at least four people injured, and authorities failed at capturing it.
items.content: A bear that roamed the streets of a Japanese city for four days, forcing mass school closures, was caught on June 9, capping a search involving dozens of hunters and police. Bear attacks have been on the rise in Japan. Last week, a bear attack in the northeastern city of Fukushima left at least four people injured, and authorities failed at capturing it.

items.title: Wild black bear captured in Japanese city after multi-day hunt
items.standfirst: Japanese authorities have captured a bear following a multi-day search that saw nearly 100 schools close.
items.content: Japanese authorities have captured a bear following a multi-day search that saw nearly 100 schools close.
